description_long: This is a classic biplane with a bright red fuselage and wings, featuring two stacked wings connected by black struts and cross-bracing wires. The nose houses a metallic, cylindrical engine cowling with a visible propeller at the front. The tail section is streamlined, ending in a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted red. The landing gear consists of two main wheels under the lower wing and a small tailwheel at the rear. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century stunt or trainer aircraft, with a robust and compact build.
description: A red biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and a front-mounted propeller, featuring classic vintage styling and black structural struts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and black struts connecting the wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, both wings, and the alignment of the landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the engine cowling, propeller, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by interplane struts and cross-bracing wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
